items.title: Nana mint Cheikhna : « la Mauritanie a besoin de réviser certaines dispositions pour garantir son avenir »
items.description: L’opposante Nana mint Cheikhna a déclaré que la Mauritanie devait réexaminer certaines questions pour assurer son avenir, notamment dans un contexte régional instable, appelant à la préservation de la paix sociale et à l'élaboration d'une feuille de route économique et politique pour le pays. Elle a ajouté, dans une déclaration à « Sahara Media » en marge de la signature du document de référence par les parties prenantes au dialogue national, mardi soir, que l’optimisme quant à l’avenir de la Mauritanie repose sur son potentiel et la diversité de sa société. Les déclarations de l’opposante font suite à la signature, à Nouakchott, du document de référence du dialogue national par les forces politiques nationales, en présence de représentants de la majorité présidentielle, de l’opposition et de la Coordination du dialogue. Le document a été signé par le coordinateur du dialogue national, Moussa Fall, ainsi que par Mohamed Bilal Messaoud au nom de la majorité présidentielle et des partis soutenant le président de la République, Hamadi Ould Sid El Mokhtar au nom de la Fondation de l’opposition démocratique, Mohamed Mouloud au nom de la Coalition de l’opposition démocratique, et Brahim Ould Beyhi au nom du Bloc des forces démocratiques. Le coordinateur du dialogue national, Moussa Fall, a déclaré que la préparation du dialogue avait duré un an et quatre mois, dont les sept premiers mois avaient été consacrés à l’élaboration d’une feuille de route à travers des consultations avec les partis politiques, les organisations de la société civile et des personnalités nationales, avant la mise au point du cadre organisationnel du dialogue. Il a ajouté que celui-ci s’organiserait autour de commissions centrales et d’ateliers portant sur quatre axes principaux, appelant les différentes parties à s’impliquer dans sa préparation.

items.title: ADB formally appointed financial, transaction adviser for outsourcing Islamabad Airport operations
items.description: ISLAMABAD: Pakistan on Wednesday formally appointed the Asian Development Bank (ADB) as the financial and transaction adviser for the outsourcing of Islamabad International Airport’s operations. Last week, the Privatisation Commission Board reviewed the terms of a transaction advisory services agreement (TASA) with the lender and gave its approval. The TASA was signed by Privatisation Commission Director General Shahid Dayo and ADB Country Director Emma Fan, an official announcement said. Adviser to the Prime Minister on Privatisation and Privatisation Commission Chairman Muhammad Ali, Privatisation Commission Secretary Usman Akhtar Bajwa, Privatisation Division Secretary Hammad Shamimi and ADB Deputy Country Director Syed Hussain Haider witnessed the signing. Under the agreement, ADB would provide comprehensive transaction advisory services, including technical, financial, legal, environmental and commercial expertise, to support the structuring and implementation of the transaction in accordance with international best practices. “The advisory services will facilitate a transparent, competitive and market-driven process aimed at attracting leading international airport operators and investors,” the Privatisation Commission said. The agreement marked an important milestone in the implementation of the government’s privatisation programme to improve the efficiency, service quality and long-term sustainability of Islamabad International Airport through private sector participation, while ensuring transparency, competitiveness and value for the people of Pakistan. “The outsourcing process is expected to be completed within nine months, while the due diligence phase is expected to conclude within three months”, a senior privatisation commission official told a Senate panel on Tuesday. Separately, a single financial advisor will be appointed for the outsourcing of Karachi and Lahore International Airports, with preparatory work already underway, he said. The agreement with the ADB marks the first instance in which a multilateral organisation will assist a privatisation transaction in the country. In February, the board formed a negotiation committee to engage with the ADB and discuss terms for a financial advisory services agreement, which then finalised a revised draft agreement with certain modifications entailing indemnity protections to the ADB to avoid litigation as a multilateral agency. The board also expressed confidence that the ADB, with its expertise and experience, would be able to successfully complete this important transaction swiftly while ensuring maximum competition and transparency. Islamabad International Airport will be outsourced to a qualified private sector operator under a long-term concession framework through a competitive bidding process. The privatisation is expected to enhance operational efficiency, improve passenger experience and align airport services with international best practices. The government is targeting the privatisation of the airport during the current fiscal year. The contract appointing ADB as transaction adviser was revised with certain changes to its terms and conditions, including indemnity clauses due to its multilateral status. The negotiated terms of the contract proposed by the Privatisation Commission were approved by the Cabinet Committee on Privatisation. The process for hiring financial advisers for outsourcing international airports in Karachi and Lahore would begin immediately, as previous government-to-government discussions had not progressed as expected. Both airports were expected to attract more than $500 million each in fresh investment, Ali told Dawn a few days ago. The board also approved the appointment of chartered accountancy firm BDO Ebrahim and Company as external auditors for the three financial years from 2025–26 to 2027–28, on the recommendation of the board’s Audit Committee. It resolved to continue ensuring transparency, efficiency and the maintenance of high standards to further strengthen investor confidence in the government’s privatisation programme, according to an official statement. In June 2024, the government made privatisation its key priority and directed that Islamabad International Airport be outsourced first .

items.title: CAN féminine 2026 : Jorge Vilda dévoilera vendredi sa liste finale
items.description: Jorge Vilda tiendra une conférence de presse vendredi 17 juillet au Complexe Mohammed VI de football à Maâmoura afin de dévoiler la liste finale des Lionnes de l'Atlas qui représenteront le Maroc lors de la Coupe d'Afrique des nations féminine 2026. La conférence débutera à 11h et permettra au sélectionneur espagnol de faire le point sur les derniers préparatifs de la sélection nationale, à quelques jours du coup d'envoi de la compétition continentale, que le Royaume accueillera du 26 juillet au 16 août. Les Marocaines aborderont ce rendez-vous avec l'ambition de décrocher un premier sacre continental devant leur public. Le tirage au sort a placé les Lionnes de l'Atlas dans le groupe A aux côtés de l'Algérie, du Sénégal et du Kenya. Le Maroc entamera son parcours face au Kenya avant d'affronter le Sénégal puis l'Algérie, avec l'objectif de terminer parmi les premières de son groupe pour accéder à la phase à élimination directe et poursuivre sa quête du titre africain. The post CAN féminine 2026 : Jorge Vilda dévoilera vendredi sa liste finale appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: 10 Samsung phones with the most storage space
items.description: For Samsung phone users who store large video files, photos, games and other content, storage capacity can be just as important as camera quality or processing power. Samsung has offered some of its most expensive flagship smartphones with up to 1TB of internal storage. That is enough space for users who record high-resolution videos, install large games and keep substantial amounts of content on their phones. Here are 10 Samsung phones with the largest storage capacities available in the Galaxy lineup. 1. Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra — Up to 1TB The Galaxy S26 Ultra is Samsung’s latest flagship smartphone and offers up to 1TB of internal storage. The 1TB version comes with 16GB of RAM, while the 256GB and 512GB versions feature 12GB of RAM. Key specifications: Maximum storage: 1TB RAM: 16GB on the 1TB model Display: 6.9-inch QHD+ Dynamic AMOLED 2X, 120Hz Processor: Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 for Galaxy Main camera: 200MP Battery: 5,000mAh 2. Samsung Galaxy Z Fold7 — Up to 1TB The Galaxy Z Fold7 combines a foldable design with up to 1TB of internal storage. The large storage option is particularly suited to users who use the foldable phone for productivity, gaming and high-resolution video recording. Key specifications: Maximum storage: 1TB RAM: Up to 16GB Main display: 8.0-inch foldable Dynamic AMOLED 2X Cover display: 6.5-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X Main camera: 200MP Battery: 4,400mAh 3. Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra — Up to 1TB The Galaxy S25 Ultra is available with up to 1TB of internal storage, making it one of Samsung’s largest-capacity smartphones. Key specifications: Maximum storage: 1TB RAM: 12GB Display: 6.9-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X, 120Hz Processor: Snapdragon 8 Elite for Galaxy Main camera: 200MP Battery: 5,000mAh 4. Samsung Galaxy Z Fold6 — Up to 1TB The Galaxy Z Fold6 offers up to 1TB of internal storage. Its foldable screen and large storage capacity make it suitable for users who want a device for both smartphone use and more demanding multitasking. Key specifications: Maximum storage: 1TB RAM: 12GB Main display: 7.6-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X, 120Hz Processor: Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 for Galaxy Main camera: 50MP Battery: 4,400mAh 5. Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra — Up to 1TB The Galaxy S24 Ultra is another Samsung flagship with a 1TB storage option. It also features the company’s S Pen, making it suitable for users who want a phone for note-taking, creative work and productivity. Key specifications: Maximum storage: 1TB RAM: 12GB Display: 6.8-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X, 120Hz Processor: Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 for Galaxy Main camera: 200MP Battery: 5,000mAh 6. Samsung Galaxy Z Fold5 — Up to 1TB The Galaxy Z Fold5 was also available with up to 1TB of internal storage, giving users substantial room for apps, photos, videos and documents. Key specifications: Maximum storage: 1TB RAM: 12GB Main display: 7.6-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X, 120Hz Processor: Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 for Galaxy Main camera: 50MP Battery: 4,400mAh 7. Samsung Galaxy Z Fold4 — Up to 1TB The Galaxy Z Fold4 became one of Samsung’s earlier foldable phones to offer a 1TB storage configuration. Key specifications: Maximum storage: 1TB RAM: 12GB Main display: 7.6-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X, up to 120Hz Processor: Snapdragon 8+ Gen 1 Main camera: 50MP Battery: 4,400mAh 8. Samsung Galaxy S23 Ultra — Up to 1TB The Galaxy S23 Ultra offers up to 1TB of internal storage and remains one of Samsung’s most powerful older flagship smartphones. Key specifications: Maximum storage: 1TB RAM: Up to 12GB Display: 6.8-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X, 120Hz Processor: Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 for Galaxy Main camera: 200MP Battery: 5,000mAh 9. Samsung Galaxy S22 Ultra — Up to 1TB The Galaxy S22 Ultra was one of Samsung’s first smartphones to offer a 1TB storage option in the S Ultra series. Key specifications: Maximum storage: 1TB RAM: Up to 12GB Display: 6.8-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X, 120Hz Processor: Snapdragon 8 Gen 1 or Exynos 2200, depending on market Main camera: 108MP Battery: 5,000mAh 10. Samsung Galaxy S21 Ultra — Up to 512GB While the Galaxy S21 Ultra does not reach the 1TB capacity offered by later Samsung flagships, its maximum 512GB internal storage still places it among the Galaxy phones with the largest storage capacities. Key specifications: Maximum storage: 512GB RAM: Up to 16GB Display: 6.8-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X, 120Hz Processor: Snapdragon 888 or Exynos 2100, depending on market Main camera: 108MP Battery: 5,000mAh Vanguard News The post 10 Samsung phones with the most storage space appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Project analyst: leadership profession – a new era of strategic excellence in project delivery
items.description: In an era defined by rapid transformation, complex investments, and increasing demands for accountability, the role of the Project Analyst has emerged as one of the most important leadership professions in modern organisations. Projects are no longer simply temporary activities managed through schedules and budgets; they are strategic vehicles that determine economic growth, institutional performance, and societal progress. At the centre of this evolution stands the Project Analyst — a professional responsible for transforming information into insight, uncertainty into clarity, and project ambitions into measurable outcomes. The Chartered Institute of Project Analysts is an independent, globally recognised, premier, and regulatory professional membership body registered under the Professional Bodies Registration Act with No. PB 112, represents a significant advancement in recognising and strengthening the Project Analyst profession. Through professional standards, ethical practice, and capacity development, the Institute provides a platform for practitioners who contribute to the success of projects across industries and sectors. The growing recognition of the Project Analyst as a leadership profession reflects a fundamental change in how organisations approach project management. Today’s leaders require more than administrative oversight. They require professionals who can interpret complex data, identify risks, evaluate performance, support strategic decisions, and ensure that projects deliver sustainable value. The Rise of the Project Analyst Leader For decades, project success was often measured by traditional indicators such as completion time, cost control, and delivery against scope. While these remain important, modern projects require a broader perspective. Organisations now operate in environments shaped by technological disruption, global competition, financial pressures, and changing stakeholder expectations. The Project Analyst has become a critical leadership partner in navigating these challenges. By combining analytical expertise with strategic understanding, Project Analysts help organisations make informed decisions throughout the project lifecycle. They provide the evidence required by executives, project managers, investors, and stakeholders to determine whether projects are aligned with organisational goals. Leadership in project analysis is not defined solely by authority or position. It is demonstrated through influence, expertise, integrity, and the ability to guide decisions. A Project Analyst leads by providing accurate information, challenging assumptions, highlighting opportunities, and promoting continuous improvement. Building Confidence Through Professional Standards The establishment of recognised professional frameworks for Project Analysts is essential to ensuring quality and credibility within the profession. As organisations commit billions of resources to projects each year, they need confidence that those responsible for analysing project performance possess the necessary competence and ethical foundation. The Chartered Institute of Project Analysts plays an important role in developing professional excellence by promoting standards that support competence, accountability, and lifelong learning. Professional recognition provides practitioners with a clear pathway for development while assuring employers and stakeholders that qualified Project Analysts operate according to established expectations. A strong profession is built on shared values. For Project Analysts, these values include accuracy, transparency, objectivity, confidentiality, and a commitment to delivering meaningful insights. These principles strengthen trust between professionals and the organisations they serve. From Data Management to Strategic Intelligence The modern Project Analyst is far more than a provider of reports. In a world where organisations generate enormous volumes of information, the true value lies in interpreting data and converting it into strategic intelligence. Project Analysts examine trends, evaluate performance indicators, forecast potential challenges, and identify areas where improvements can be made. Their work enables leaders to act proactively rather than reactively. They help answer essential questions: Are resources being used effectively? Are risks being managed appropriately? Is the project creating the intended value? This analytical leadership contributes directly to better governance and stronger decision-making. Whether supporting infrastructure development, technology programmes, public sector initiatives, financial investments, or organisational change, Project Analysts provide the insight required to achieve successful outcomes. The Importance of Ethical and Responsible Leadership As the influence of Project Analysts continues to grow, ethical leadership becomes increasingly important. Decisions based on inaccurate analysis or incomplete information can have significant consequences for organisations, communities, and economies. Professional Project Analysts must therefore uphold the highest standards of honesty and responsibility. Their role requires independence of thought and the courage to present objective findings, even when those findings challenge expectations. True professional leadership is not about producing favourable answers; it is about providing accurate answers that support better decisions. The Chartered Institute of Project Analysts’ commitment to professional development and ethical practice reinforces the importance of building a trusted community of specialists capable of supporting responsible project delivery. Preparing the Next Generation of Project Leaders The future of projects will depend on professionals who can combine analytical capability with leadership vision. As artificial intelligence, automation, and advanced technologies reshape workplaces, the role of the Project Analyst will continue to evolve. Technology will enhance the ability to collect and process information, but human judgement will remain essential. Project Analysts will be required to interpret complex situations, understand organisational priorities, communicate effectively, and support leaders in making balanced decisions. Investment in education, certification, mentoring, and continuous professional development will be critical in preparing the next generation of Project Analysts. By strengthening professional pathways, the industry can ensure that emerging practitioners are equipped to meet the challenges of increasingly complex projects. A Profession Shaping the Future The recognition of the Project Analyst as a leadership profession marks an important milestone in the development of project excellence. As organisations seek greater efficiency, transparency, and value from their investments, the expertise of Project Analysts will become increasingly indispensable. The Chartered Institute of Project Analysts, an independent, globally recognised, premier, and regulatory professional membership body registered under the Professional Bodies Registration Act with No. PB 112, stands at the forefront of this professional advancement. Its commitment to establishing standards, supporting practitioners, and promoting excellence reflects the growing importance of analytical leadership in the project environment. The future belongs to organisations that can make informed decisions, adapt quickly, and deliver meaningful results. Project Analysts are uniquely positioned to support this future by providing the knowledge, insight, and leadership required to transform ideas into successful realities. The Project Analyst is no longer simply a supporting function. It is a leadership profession — one that helps organisations understand their challenges, seize opportunities, and achieve sustainable success.

items.title: Bassar : 1000 verres correcteurs aux personnes souffrant de problèmes de vision
items.description: Bassar, 15 juil. (ATOP) – Mille verres correcteurs ont été distribués aux personnes nécessiteuses ayant des problèmes de vision, lors d’une campagne les 13 et 14 juillet à Bassar. Une patiente regardant dans l’auto-réf La campagne initiée par l’église des Assemblées de Dieu (A/D), temple la Grâce de Kpankissi (commune Bassar 1), entre dans le cadre de la célébration de son 75ᵉ anniversaire. Elle a été faite en collaboration avec la structure médicale Christ (All Nation Every Tribe) ANET-Mission basée à Lomé en partenariat avec le ministère en charge de la Santé et le Programme national de santé oculaire. A travers cette action, les initiateurs entendent accompagner la population souffrant des maux d’yeux et ne disposant pas des moyens de se faire traiter. Les patients ont été consultés au Centre hospitalier préfectoral (CHP) de Bassar, des analyses ont permis de recenser les personnes souffrant de la cataracte, des soins visuels ont été offerts à cette population vulnérable. Les patients ont bénéficié des verres correcteurs. Le pasteur de l’église des A/D, temple la Grâce de Kpankissi, Didier Kodjo Houndjafo a demandé aux bénéficiaires de prendre soin des verres offerts. Il a exhorté l’ensemble de la population à prier pour le peuple Bassar ainsi que pour ses dirigeants. Des verres correcteurs Le chef d’équipe de ANET-Mission, Mayatchi Souley, a souligné que leur mission consiste à aider les populations nécessiteuses, surtout celles de l’intérieur du pays, à avoir des soins de santé de qualité, et à sortir de la précarité. Il a prodigué des conseils aux bénéficiaires concernant l’entretien des verres et les aptitudes à éviter pour garder les yeux en bon état. Les bénéficiaires, satisfaits, ont souhaité que cette opération soit élargie à toute la préfecture de Bassar et à d’autres localités du pays. « Je suis très content aujourd’hui. Grâce aux verres qui m’ont été remis, je pourrai désormais mieux voir », a laissé entendre un patient. ATOP/LK/BV/KYA

items.title: Nigeria needs more investment in agriculture to tackle hunger — BUA’s Rabiu
items.description: …As BUA Foods Records ₦518.4bn Profit After Tax By Emma Ujah, Abuja Bureau Chief The Chairman of BUA Foods Plc, Alhaji Abdul Samad Rabiu, has identified increased investment in agriculture by both government and private sector players as a major solution to tackling hunger and food insecurity in Nigeria. Rabiu spoke at the company’s 5th Annual General Meeting held in Abuja on Wednesday, where BUA Foods announced a Profit After Tax (PAT) of ₦518.4 billion for the financial year ended December 2025, compared with ₦265.9 billion recorded in the previous year. The BUA Foods chairman said while the company was pleased with its financial performance, it remained conscious of its role in a critical sector that contributes significantly to national development. He said the company’s responsibility goes beyond creating value for shareholders to building a stronger and more competitive food industry that supports Nigeria’s long-term economic growth. “We have a responsibility not only to create value for our shareholders but also to help build a stronger, more competitive industry that contributes to Nigeria’s long-term growth,” he said. Rabiu said the company’s planned expansion was aimed at strengthening Nigeria’s food production capacity and positioning BUA Foods as the leading player in the sector. “By next year, when our current projects are completed, BUA will become the largest player in our sector in Nigeria. “We are not pursuing growth merely for the sake of becoming bigger. We are pursuing growth because scale matters in an industry like ours,” he said. He expressed confidence in the company’s future, attributing its performance to discipline, efficiency and the commitment of its workforce. “I am pleased to report that the business is doing very well. Our performance this year reflects the discipline, efficiency and hard work of our people across the organisation. We have delivered strong results, and we remain confident about the future,” he added. Rabiu noted that four companies currently control about 90 per cent of Nigeria’s food industry, adding that BUA Foods remained the only indigenous company among them. He said the board and management were determined to strengthen the company’s position and become a dominant force in the sector. Speaking in a post-AGM interview, Non-Executive Director of BUA Foods, Alhaji Kabiru Rabiu, stressed the need for massive investments in agriculture by both government and private sector operators to address food insecurity. He called for the provision of farm inputs, including fertilisers and herbicides, to farmers at subsidised rates to improve agricultural productivity. Kabiru said BUA Foods had taken steps in that direction but urged other stakeholders to increase their involvement through partnerships with governments at all levels. He identified insecurity as one of the major challenges affecting agricultural production but commended government efforts in addressing the problem. According to him, Nigeria must also increase investment in irrigation farming and reduce overdependence on rain-fed agriculture to ensure food production throughout the year. He said countries that had achieved food security relied heavily on irrigation systems to sustain agricultural output. Meanwhile, BUA Foods reported a total revenue of ₦1.77 trillion for the period under review, compared with ₦1.53 trillion recorded in 2024. The post Nigeria needs more investment in agriculture to tackle hunger — BUA’s Rabiu appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: 3 arrested in connection with young doctor's killing during robbery near Karachi's Teen Talwar
items.description: KARACHI: Three suspects have been arrested in connection with the murder of a young doctor who was shot dead during a robbery near Teen Talwar in Karachi’s Clifton area, according to a press release issued by South Zone police on Wednesday. Dr Akash Kumar, a doctor at the Jinnah Postgraduate Medical Centre (JPMC), was killed on Monday after robbers intercepted his vehicle near Teen Talwar and escaped with around Rs2 million that he was carrying after withdrawing cash from a private bank. The killing sparked widespread outrage within the medical community, with doctors staging protests at the Jinnah Postgraduate Medical Centre (JPMC), where Dr Akash served as a house officer. The Pakistan Medical Association (PMA) also condemned the killing, describing it as evidence of the deteriorating law and order situation in Karachi. In the press release issued on Wednesday, police provided details of the events leading up to the robbery. They said that Dr Akash arrived at a bank on Monday to withdraw Rs5m, of which he kept Rs2m and Rs3m in separate white envelopes. It said that the vehicle with Dr Akash, his father, a cousin and the cash envelopes was intercepted by four robbers on two motorcycles when they arrived at their location, which was a second bank. It said that one of the suspects opened the car door and fired at Dr Akash, who then died after being shifted to the hospital. It said that a case of the incident was subsequently registered on the complaint of the suspect’s father. The police said that on Tuesday night, a suspicious car was seen in Defence area. It said that officials pursued them and arrested three suspects, adding that mobile phones and three pistols were also seized, for which a case was registered. It added that a white Suzuki Alto allegedly used by the suspects to flee, bearing fake registration plates, was also seized. According to the press release, the suspects told investigators during initial interrogation that they were involved in the doctor’s killing. They said that they had monitored the victim before the robbery and had an accomplice inside the first bank, who alerted them after Dr Akash withdrew the cash. Police said the insider informed the gang about the victim’s appearance and the amount withdrawn, enabling them to intercept the vehicle as it headed to the second bank. Police said in the press release that the arrested suspects belonged to a gang involved in robberies and street crimes across Karachi and had criminal records, adding that raids were under way to arrest their remaining accomplices. The press release was issued after a press conference by South Deputy Inspector General (DIG) Asad Raza and Muqaddas Haider, the Crime Investigation Agency’s (CIA) DIG in Karachi. The officials also shared the details outlined in the press release during the media briefing in Karachi, where DIG Raza said police had collected more than 100 files from CCTV cameras installed in different areas to track down the suspects. “It helped us unearth the identity of the suspects and arrest them,” he said, adding that one of the arrested suspects was recently released on bail. He said another arrested suspect was facing trial in a case and the third one had been declared an absconder. DIG Raza identified the arrested suspects as Suresh, Ram Chand and Anil, who he said had been residing in Karachi for a considerable time but hailed from Daharki, Thul and Naushahro Feroze. Replying to a question, he ruled out personal enmity as a reason for the murder. CIA DIG Haider said 30 cases of robbery with murder had been reported in Karachi to the law enforcers so far this year, in comparison to 48 cases reported during the first seven months of 2025. “This is a decline of at least 37 per cent,” he said, adding that cases of robbery with injury had shown more than 30pc decline. Acknowledging that street crime was a big issue, he maintained that no rise was recorded in those cases this year in comparison to last year and were “still under control”. He also said that vehicle snatching had been on a “continuous decline” since last year. He refuted that street crime was on the rise in the city. On this, DIG Raza said a spike in cases of robbery with murder and robbery with injury was seen in January and May, but just one case of robbery with murder and 18 of robbery with injury were recorded in June. “The number is significantly less than the one recorded in previous months and these cases are showing a declining trend,” he said. Later, Sindh Home Minister Ziaul Hassan Lanjar said in a statement that the provincial government was committed to ensuring justice was served in the case. He said he would personally oversee the case’s investigation and issued directives for the Karachi additional inspector general to beef up security in all upscale and sensitive areas of the city, increase police patrolling, and intensify targeted operations against criminal elements. He also announced that he would conduct surprise visits across the city to personally review the law and order situation. “There will be no compromise on law and order, and the provincial government is utilising all available administrative and legal resources to safeguard citizens,” he assured. He said efforts were under way to arrest other suspects and facilitators in the case. The FIR A case for Dr Akash’s murder was registered at Karachi’s Frere Hall Police Station on Tuesday, according to a copy of the first information report (FIR) seen by Dawn . The FIR was registered on the complaint of Dr Akash’s cousin, Sanjay Kumar, under Sections 34 (acts done by several persons in furtherance of a common intention), 397 (robbery or dacoity, with attempt to cause death or grievous hurt) and 302 (murder) of the Pakistan Penal Code. The complainant told the police that he, Dr Akash and his father had reached a bank at around 11:40am on July 13 and drawn cash worth Rs50m. He said Dr Akash’s father divided the cash, keeping an envelope containing Rs30m with him and handing over the one with Rs20m to Dr Akash as they left for another bank. Kumar informed the police that Dr Akash’s father was sitting on the passenger seat beside him in the car while Dr Akash was on the back seat. According to his account of the incident, the culprits, on two motorcycles, accosted their vehicle near the second bank. “They stopped one of the motorcycles beside the window of the driver’s seat and the other behind my car,” he said, adding that one of the men on the motorcycles first fired at the bank’s guard, who remained unhurt. “Similarly, one among them hit their weapon on the driver’s seat window and fired a shot at me, but I, too, remained unhurt,” he added. They then opened the back seat door and fired at Dr Akash, who was wounded and later succumbed to his injuries, the complainant said. He added that the robbers took the Rs2m envelope kept with Dr Akash and fled the scene. He also said he would be able to identify them.

items.title: Senate committee raises concern over NCDC funding shortfall, commends management
items.description: By Golok Nanmwa JOS — The Senate Committee on the North Central Development Commission (NCDC) has expressed concern over the slow and inadequate release of funds to the Commission, warning that the shortfall could affect its ability to effectively deliver on its mandate. The committee, however, commended the Governing Board and Management of the Commission for laying a strong institutional foundation despite the funding challenges. The position was contained in a statement signed by the Head of Media and Corporate Communications of the Commission, Dr. Makut Simon Macham, and made available to journalists on Wednesday. According to the statement, Chairman of the Senate Committee, Senator Titus Zam, spoke after the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of the Commission, Dr. Cyril Yiltsen Tsenyil, led the management team to brief lawmakers on the Commission’s activities since its establishment. Zam said the committee was satisfied with the efforts of the Commission’s leadership in building the necessary structures required to drive development across the North-Central region. He noted that despite the limited release of funds, which he said was far below the approved budget, the committee was impressed by the Commission’s vision, prudent management and commitment to implementing projects that would improve the lives of people in the region. “We invited the management to take stock of what they have done since inauguration and the passage of the budget. “Having listened to the Managing Director and his team, we are satisfied with the efforts being made to place the Commission on the right footing. “Despite the slow release of funds, which is far below the approved budget, we are encouraged by the prudent management and vision demonstrated by the Commission. We will engage the relevant authorities to ensure more funds are released for it to achieve meaningful results,” Zam said. The senator assured that the committee would engage relevant government authorities to facilitate the release of additional funds to enable the Commission to effectively execute its mandate. He also disclosed that members of the committee would soon embark on an oversight visit to the Commission’s headquarters in Lafia, Nasarawa State. Earlier, Tsenyil appreciated President Bola Tinubu for establishing the NCDC and the National Assembly for providing the legislative backing and approving budgets for the Commission. He said the Commission had commenced institutional activities through partnerships with key Ministries, Departments and Agencies (MDAs), development partners and state governments across the North-Central region. According to him, the Commission had engaged the governments of Nasarawa, Plateau, Kogi and Benue states, all of which pledged support and collaboration. Tsenyil disclosed that the Nasarawa State Government had donated an office complex in Lafia to serve as the Commission’s headquarters, while other states had also pledged office accommodation and logistical support. He further revealed that Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) with the Rural Electrification Agency (REA), Small and Medium Enterprises Development Agency of Nigeria (SMEDAN), and Nigeria Incentive-Based Risk Sharing System for Agricultural Lending (NIRSAL) had reached the final stage of legal vetting ahead of formal signing. According to him, the partnerships are aimed at promoting empowerment, agricultural development and economic growth across the region. The NCDC boss said the Commission had begun utilising available funds to establish its operational structures and carry out other foundational activities. He added that procurement processes and Bureau of Public Procurement (BPP) “No Objection” approvals had been secured for priority projects captured in the 2025 budget, paving the way for implementation. Tsenyil also disclosed that the Board had approved the Commission’s Strategic Development Roadmap, which would be unveiled at the maiden North Central Regional Stakeholders Development Summit. He added that the Board had approved the commencement of the process of incorporating the NCDC Investment Company to promote business development and attract alternative sources of funding for the Commission. The post Senate committee raises concern over NCDC funding shortfall, commends management appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Achimota Forest protection commendable, but galamsey fight must target masterminds — Awula Serwah
items.description: Coordinator of Eco-Conscious Citizens, Awula Serwah, has welcomed the revocation of Executive Instrument (EI) 144, which restores the Achimota Forest Reserve to its full protected status. She described it as a major victory for years of advocacy to protect Ghana’s forest reserves. However, she says the government’s fight against illegal mining will remain incomplete unless authorities move beyond arresting small-scale operators and prosecute the powerful individuals and politically exposed persons allegedly behind galamsey activities. Speaking on JoyNews’ The Pulse on Wednesday, July 15, Madam Serwah said the restoration of the Achimota Forest Reserve to its protected status was a positive development, given the ecological importance of the forest to Accra. Read Also: Cabinet restores Achimota Forest Reserve to full protected status “We are pleased that EI 144 has finally been revoked. We know that Achimota Forest is the lungs of Accra, the last remaining forest reserve in Greater Accra. So we are pleased that EI 144 has been revoked,” she said. While praising the revocation of EI 144, she called on government to take further steps to recover portions of the Achimota Forest Reserve that had previously been allocated to private individuals and reintegrate them into the protected reserve. She also commended the earlier revocation of Legislative Instrument (LI) 2462, which removed the legal basis for mining in forest reserves. Despite these gains, Madam Serwah expressed concern about the pace of prosecutions in the fight against illegal mining, questioning why prominent individuals suspected of financing or facilitating galamsey activities have not been held accountable. “Apart from Chairman Wontumi being prosecuted, which politically exposed persons are being prosecuted?” she asked. According to her, while several people have been arrested in connection with illegal mining, many of those appearing before the courts are ordinary citizens, while the alleged financiers and influential actors remain untouched. “We have the ordinary Kwame and Kojo being prosecuted, but those behind the big boys behind illegal mining are not being arrested and prosecuted,” she stated. Madam Serwah referenced President John Mahama’s earlier comment that some members of his own government could be involved in illegal mining, saying such allegations must be followed with investigations and accountability. She stressed that Ghana’s forests and water bodies continue to face an existential threat from illegal mining and urged authorities to ensure that enforcement efforts target all levels of the illegal mining chain. His remarks follow the Minister’s announcement that Cabinet had revoked EI 144, restoring the Achimota Forest Reserve to its full protected status and reversing the decision that excised part of the forest from the reserve four years ago. The revocation restores the forest to its original classification under the 1927 declaration, effectively nullifying the legal changes introduced in 2022 that removed approximately 361.5 acres from the southern portion of the reserve.

items.title: Begoña Gómez entrega al juez Peinado los billetes de su viaje a Londres para la graduación de su hija
items.description: Begoña Gómez ha entregado al juzgado los billetes de avión de su viaje a Londres de la pasada semana y ha presentado un recurso contra la iniciativa del magistrado Juan Carlos Peinado de reclamarle que “acredite” que solo usó el pasaporte para acudir a Reino Unido a la graduación de su hija. En su escrito de alegaciones, la defensa de la esposa de Pedro Sánchez, ejercida por el exministro socialista Antonio Camacho, recuerda que el país británico ya no pone un “sello físico” al acceder. Y reprocha al magistrado que le inste a demostrar “la inexistencia de una conducta delictiva”: “La carga de la prueba corresponde a la acusación”. Seguir leyendo

items.title: Trump tells immigration agents to keep traffic stops despite killings
items.description: US President Donald Trump lashed out Wednesday at a decision by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to suspend the use of traffic stops after two fatal shootings in less than a week. The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) halted the practice after a Colombian man was shot dead in Maine on Monday and a Mexican man was killed in an operation in Texas last week. “We CANNOT give up one of I.C.E.’s most important and effective Crime Fighting tools, THE TRAFFIC STOP!” Trump said in an early morning post on his Truth Social network. “Once we do, we are playing right into the criminal’s hands. The Radical Left Dumocrats would like to see this done, but it won’t happen on my watch. I.C.E., be judicious, fair and smart, and go back and do your very important job.” Trump’s border czar Tom Homan told reporters at the White House on Tuesday that there was a “pause” in traffic stops, but insisted the practice was effective and would return. Tasked with enforcing Trump’s immigration crackdown, ICE’s heavily armed agents have faced nationwide backlash for aggressive tactics and for the shooting deaths of two US citizens earlier this year in Minneapolis. Rights groups identified the victim in Monday’s shooting in Biddeford, Maine as Joan Sebastian Guerrero, 26, a delivery driver authorized to work in the United States, who lived with his wife and three-year-old daughter. Colombian leader Gustavo Petro, a harsh critic of Trump’s immigration crackdown, called the killing a “murder of a Latin American Colombian at the hands of the US government.” In last week’s shooting in Texas, immigration authorities claimed Lorenzo Salgado, 52, had tried to run over an ICE agent but witnesses have disputed that account. The post Trump tells immigration agents to keep traffic stops despite killings appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Media shaped American Revolution and birth of modern democracy — Havard Lecturer, Prof Christopher Rhodes
items.description: A senior lecturer in social sciences at Boston University and lecturer in the Department of Government at Harvard University, Prof Christopher Rhodes, says the media was a key force in driving the American Revolution and shaping the foundations of modern democracy. Delivering the keynote address virtually on the opening day of the State of Journalism, Media, and Communication Conference 2026 (SJMCC 2026) on Wednesday, July 15, Prof Rhodes argued that the contribution of journalists to the struggle for liberty has often been overlooked despite its historical significance. Speaking on the theme “Beyond Enemy of the People: Journalism, Civic Culture, Media Partisanship, and Democratic Renewal in an Age of Democratic Backsliding,” he said the role of newspapers and journalists in securing democratic freedoms deserves greater recognition. “I started to research the role of journalists and journalism in the American Revolution,” he said. “And from there, I decided to explore the role of journalism and journalists in the Indian independence struggle as well. These two struggles for freedom are not only the birth moments of our two respective nations, but they are pivotal moments in world history.” According to him, journalism served as a powerful force in advancing freedom, democracy and independence long before the establishment of modern democratic institutions. “Among the various anniversaries we’ve celebrated and the histories we’ve compiled, the roles of journalism have often been underappreciated.” Drawing on historical accounts, Prof Rhodes explained that newspapers in the American colonies became powerful instruments for mobilising public opinion against British colonial rule. “The ability of these newspapers to freely criticise the colonial government came from liberal policies and permissive applications of the law that created an atmosphere of free press within the colonies.” He noted that the influence of newspapers extended far beyond their readership, as information was widely shared by word of mouth. “Because the news from the press was shared in oral form, the written word was spread much deeper into society and was probably more powerful than any one form of media message today.” Prof Rhodes also pointed to Britain’s Stamp Act as a turning point that galvanised newspapers against colonial rule. “This created a situation in which you had a press, which on the one hand was free from government restrictions, but on the other hand was now threatened by government taxation.” “And so that press was partisan and fiercely anti-royalist, both out of principle and out of self-interest.” He said newspapers continued to rally support for the revolutionary cause even after the repeal of the Stamp Act, with press freedom eventually becoming a constitutional right. “Freedom of the press was seen as so fundamental to the United States and to democracy that it was enshrined in the First Amendment of the U.S. Constitution.”

items.title: Ansongo : la semaine scolaire met la paix et le vivre-ensemble à l’honneur
items.description: Plus d'une centaine de personnes, dont des enseignants, eleves, responsables scolaires et autorites administratives, ont participe a la semaine scolaire et artistique organisee a Ansongo. Placee sous le theme de la paix, de la cohesion sociale et du vivre-ensemble, cette activite a donne lieu a des prestations de poesie, de chant, de choregraphie et de theâtre. Les meilleurs eleves ont recu des kits scolaires. Organisee par le Centre d'animation pedagogique d'Ansongo avec le soutien de l'UNICEF, la rencontre a ete saluee par le prefet, qui a encourage les eleves a promouvoir un Mali uni et en paix. L’article Ansongo : la semaine scolaire met la paix et le vivre-ensemble à l'honneur est apparu en premier sur Studio Tamani - Informations, débats, magazines : toute l’actualité du Mali, en 5 langues .

items.title: Sotouboua : Des éleveurs de porcs renforcent leurs compétences en alimentation et gestion des porcheries
items.description: Sotouboua, 15 juil. (ATOP) – Trente éleveurs de porcs de la préfecture de Sotouboua prennent part, du 13 au 17 juillet dans leur localité, à une formation sur la valorisation des aliments locaux dans l’alimentation porcine et les techniques modernes de gestion des porcheries. La session est initiée par l’Union préfectorale des organisations paysannes de Sotouboua (UPOPS) en collaboration avec le Centre de représentation et d’exportation des produits (CREP-Togo). Elle bénéficie de l’appui technique et financier du Fonds national d’apprentissage, de formation et de perfectionnement professionnel (FNAFPP). La formation s’inscrit dans le cadre de la vulgarisation des bonnes pratiques agricoles et de la promotion de la filière porcine. Elle vise à renforcer les capacités techniques des producteurs afin d’accroître leurs performances et de promouvoir des pratiques d’élevage plus modernes. La session entend également favoriser l’utilisation des ressources alimentaires locales dans la fabrication des rations, tout en renforçant les mesures de biosécurité pour prévenir les épizooties, notamment la peste porcine africaine. Les participants suivront quatre modules portant sur les races porcines, l’habitat et la santé animale, la formulation d’aliments à base de ressources locales ainsi que la gestion d’une porcherie et d’une activité génératrice de revenus. Les formateurs vont présenter les possibilités de valorisation des sous-produits agricoles, des résidus de récolte, des tubercules et des céréales disponibles dans la préfecture pour élaborer des aliments équilibrés destinés aux porcs. Ils vont également insister sur le respect des règles d’hygiène, la désinfection régulière des installations et l’aménagement adéquat des porcheries afin de prévenir les maladies, d’améliorer le bien-être des animaux et d’accroître la rentabilité des élevages. Le secrétaire général de la préfecture de Sotouboua, Akakpo Nyagoulamba, a rappelé que l’élevage constitue un important levier de développement économique, de création d’emplois et de lutte contre la pauvreté. Il a invité les participants à appliquer les connaissances acquises pour améliorer la gestion sanitaire et économique de leurs exploitations. Le directeur du cabinet de formation CREP-Togo, Kadayi N’sougan, et le président de l’UPOPS, Kili Lazabalo, ont souligné que le développement de la filière porcine représente une véritable opportunité d’amélioration des revenus des ménages ruraux. Ils ont exhorté les éleveurs à mettre en pratique les enseignements reçus afin de moderniser leurs exploitations et de renforcer leur compétitivité. ATOP/BTP/MEK/MG

items.title: The Atlantic Announces Marc Novicoff and Nancy Walecki as Staff Writers, Michael Zelenko as Senior Editor, and James Kirchick as Contributing Writer
items.description: The Atlantic is announcing four new roles and promotions in the newsroom: Marc Novicoff and Nancy Walecki as staff writers, both moving from editor roles at The Atlantic ; Michael Zelenko as a senior editor, joining from Rest of World ; and James Kirchick as a contributing writer, who has written for The Atlantic as a freelancer since 2008. More details from our editors about all four journalists follow: Marc Novicoff is becoming a staff writer. Marc joined us in 2024 as an assistant editor, a role he performed so well that one writer said that Marc had conducted “the best fact check ever,” a claim that itself has not been checked. As a writer, Marc has demonstrated both a great sense for Atlantic stories and a willingness to jump on assignments. He has pursued a wide range of curiosities—including prediction markets, the birth-rate collapse, and the gerrymandering wars—and his recent coverage of New York City’s out-of-control education budget generated a mini-news cycle of its own. Nancy Walecki is being promoted to staff writer. In her time here, Nancy has shown remarkable gifts as a journalist and narrative stylist, capable of telling beautiful, heartbreaking, and deeply human stories. On top of her work as a fast and discerning fact-checker, she has written movingly about her own experiences: Her feature about her father’s music store captured the spirit both of her dad and of the era in music that he helped shape, and her coverage of the L.A. fires, including the loss of her family friend, brought home the devastation of the disaster. More recently, she has explored with an empathic, sharp eye the ways Americans are trying to live better, whether by perfecting their toddlers’ beauty routines, hiring a house manager, or freeing themselves from algorithms in order to embrace God. She also went on a mission , alongside Grace Buono, to find the East Wing rubble. Michael Zelenko is joining us as a senior editor. Michael has spent more than a decade directing coverage of the biggest stories in technology. He comes to us from Rest of World , where he was an executive editor. He was previously the executive editor at Medium’s OneZero and a senior features editor at The Verge . Michael has assigned groundbreaking stories about all the ways new technologies have changed how we live, work, and interact with the world, and about the powerful, eccentric figures behind these technologies. Most recently, his work has focused on AI, electric vehicles, and China’s fraught presence in America’s tech ecosystem. We’re happy to welcome him as we continue a major expansion of our coverage of tech and AI. James Kirchick is joining us as a contributing writer. He is a brilliant thinker who has written with verve and a notable fearlessness on a wide range of topics, including American and European politics, illiberal regimes, and the historical origins of today’s political movements. With typical relentlessness, he recently secured one of the last interviews with the now-late Barney Frank, and wrote about the Democratic congressman’s final message to his party. Jamie is the author of The End of Europe and of Secret City: The Hidden History of Gay Washington and has previously written for The New York Times , The Wall Street Journal , Commentary , The Free Press, and many other publications. Press Contacts: Paul Jackson and Quinn O’Brien | press@theatlantic.com

items.title: Boulgou/Education : L’école de Tengsoba “B” célèbre l’excellence scolaire avec 100% de réussite
items.description: Boulgou/Education : L’école de Tengsoba “B” célèbre l’excellence scolaire avec 100% de réussite Niaogho, 14 juillet 2026 (AIB) – L’école primaire publique de Tengsoba “B” a officiellement clôturé ses activités pédagogiques de l’année scolaire 2025-2026, ce mardi. La cérémonie, marquée par la distinction des meilleurs élèves, a été l’occasion de célébrer un taux de réussite exceptionnel de 100% au Certificat d’études primaires (CEP). L’ambiance était à la fête, ce mardi, dans l’enceinte de l’établissement. Parents d’élèves, enseignants, autorités locales et partenaires de l’éducation se sont réunis pour couronner une année de dur labeur, soldée par un succès éclatant. Le triomphe du mérite et de l’effort Sur les 68 candidats régulièrement inscrits, dont 33 garçons et 35 filles, ainsi que les 2 candidats libres présentés par l’école, tous ont décroché leur précieux sésame. Pour récompenser cette quête de l’excellence, la direction de l’école a primé les 30 meilleurs élèves de l’année, soit les 5 premiers de chaque classe, du CP1 au CM2. Ces lauréats ont reçu des kits scolaires composés de sacs à dos, de cahiers, de livres et de stylos. La directrice de l’école, Rahinata Kouanda, a exprimé sa profonde gratitude à l’ensemble des acteurs du système éducatif local qui ont œuvré pour l’obtention de ces résultats. Elle a toutefois plaidé pour un soutien encore plus accru des parents d’élèves pour les années à venir, afin de maintenir ce cap de l’excellence. Un appel partagé par le parrain de la cérémonie, Issouf Guebré. Tout en témoignant sa reconnaissance à l’équipe enseignante pour son abnégation, il a exhorté les parents à rester mobilisés aux côtés des encadreurs : « L’éducation est une œuvre collective. Les enseignants font leur part, mais le suivi à la maison par les parents est le ciment qui consolide ces excellents résultats », a-t-il souligné. Présent à la cérémonie, le représentant du Président de la délégation spéciale (PDS) de Niaogho, Jérémy Sinamba, a transmis les félicitations de l’autorité communale à toute l’équipe pédagogique qui a su « braver les difficultés » pour hisser l’établissement au sommet. Il les a encouragés à poursuivre dans cette dynamique d’excellence. Cap sur la rentrée 2026-2027 L’école de Tengsoba “B” ne s’est pas contentée de célébrer les résultats. La direction et les parents d’élèves ont mis à profit cette tribune pour choisir officiellement la tenue scolaire de l’année académique 2026-2027. Ce choix s’inscrit en droite ligne des directives gouvernementales visant à harmoniser et valoriser le port du pagne tissé ou du textile local en milieu scolaire. C’est dans une ambiance festive et conviviale, ponctuée de pas de danse et de séances photos, que les acteurs de l’école de Tengsoba “B” se sont donné rendez-vous pour la rentrée prochaine, avec le défi d’égaler, sinon de surpasser, ce score parfait. Agence d’Information du Burkina AA/SM/ata

items.title: Ogou : Les opérations de distribution de moustiquaires démarrées dans les Plateaux-Est
items.description: Atakpamé, 15 juil. (ATOP) – La campagne nationale de distribution de Moustiquaires imprégnées d’insecticides (MII) a débuté le mardi 14 juillet dans les différentes préfectures de la région des Plateaux-Est. Ces distributions ont démarré en présence des acteurs de la santé publique et des autorités locales. Sur le terrain, la distribution s’appuie sur une technologie moderne. Les agents disposent des terminaux pour scanner les coupons de dotation des ménages ainsi que le code QR unique apposé sur chaque moustiquaire, garantissant ainsi la traçabilité et l’équité de la répartition. Vue partielle de la distribution à Datcha Dans la préfecture de l’Akébou, le Comité local d’organisation (CLO), conduit par le préfet Toyi Kokou, a entrepris une tournée d’évaluation dans plusieurs postes stratégiques, notamment à Kougnohou, Djitramé et Djon. Cette inspection vise à garantir la fluidité des opérations et à désamorcer d’éventuelles contraintes logistiques sur le terrain. Les préfectures de l’Ogou, Anié, Amou, Wawa et de l’Akébou ont enregistré une forte mobilisation des populations le premier jour des opérations. Celles du Haho, Moyen-Mono et de l’Est-Mono ont mis au point tous les dispositifs pour atteindre les ménages les plus reculés. Les autorités sanitaires, encouragées par cette première journée d’affluence, ont exhorté les populations de toutes les préfectures des Plateaux-Est à se rendre massivement dans les points de distribution avant la clôture officielle de la campagne prévue samedi prochain. ATOP/KKT/BA/KYA

items.title: Sur instructions royales, le Maroc signe l’Accord de participation à l'ISF à Gaza
items.description: En exécution des hautes instructions du Roi, Chef Suprême et Chef d’Etat Major Général des Forces Armées Royales (FAR) et Président du Comité Al Qods, le inistre des Affaires Etrangères, de la Coopération Africaine et des Marocains Résidant à l’Etranger, Nasser Bourita, et le ministre délégué chargé de l’Administration de la Défense Nationale, Abdeltif Loudyi, ont reçu, en présence du Général de Corps d’Armée, Inspecteur Général des FAR et Commandant la Zone Sud et du Général de Corps d’Armée, Commandant la Gendarmerie Royale, mercredi au siège de cette Administration, Nickolay Mladenov, Haut représentant du Conseil de paix pour Gaza à la tête d’une délégation de haut niveau composée des responsables de ce Conseil et du Commandant de la Force Internationale de Stabilisation (ISF) à Gaza. Un communiqué de l'Administration de la Défense nationale précise que cette visite, qui s’inscrit dans le cadre de la concrétisation des engagements du Royaume du Maroc, en tant que membre fondateur du Conseil de Paix, conformément aux hautes directives royales, annoncées lors de la réunion inaugurale du Conseil de Paix tenue à Washington sous la présidence du Président des Etats-Unis d’Amérique, Donald Trump, a été marquée par la signature de l’Accord relatif à la participation du Royaume du Maroc à la Force Internationale de Stabilisation à Gaza. Lors de cette rencontre, la partie marocaine a souligné que la participation du Maroc à l’ISF témoigne de l’attachement profond du Royaume aux valeurs de paix, de coopération et de solidarité internationale et contribuera à la réalisation et l’instauration de la paix et la sécurité dans la région du Moyen-Orient. Elle a ensuite mis en avant l’expérience accumulée par le Royaume du Maroc dans le cadre des opérations de maintien de la paix sous l’égide de l’ONU ainsi que son engagement constant aussi bien au niveau régional qu’international pour la paix et la stabilité en mettant en relief les initiatives royales lancées par le Roi, particulièrement les projets d’intégration régionale qui impactent la sécurité et le progrès social. De leur côté, les hauts responsables du Conseil de paix pour Gaza et le Commandant de l’ISF ont salué vivement l’adhésion du Royaume du Maroc à cette initiative de paix par sa contribution à travers le déploiement d’officiers de haut rang des FAR auprès du Commandement Conjoint de l’ISF, des cadres de la Gendarmerie Royale et de la DGSN ainsi que la mise sur pied d’un hôpital militaire de campagne. Au terme de cette rencontre, les deux parties ont procédé à la signature de l’Accord relatif à la participation du Royaume du Maroc à la Force Internationale de Stabilisation à Gaza qui constitue le cadre juridique couvrant les aspects technico-opérationnels de cette participation et traduit la volonté commune de contribuer par des actions concrètes humanitaires et sécuritaires à la construction d’un climat de paix et de sécurité dans cette région. The post Sur instructions royales, le Maroc signe l’Accord de participation à l'ISF à Gaza appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Les résultats des concours d’entrée en 1ère année du collège et ceux du brevet attendus aujourd’hui
items.description: La ministre de l'Éducation et de la Réforme du système éducatif en Mauritanie, Houda Babah, a déclaré que les résultats des deux concours nationaux d'admission en première année de collège (« Concours ») et de fin de collège (« brevet ») devraient être annoncés aujourd'hui. La ministre a ajouté dans son discours que l’organisation de ces deux concours avait cette année fait l’objet d’une plus grande rigueur dans certains centres, dans le cadre de la mise en œuvre de nouvelles mesures de lutte contre la tricherie. Elle a précisé que ces mesures se sont répercutées sur les taux de réussite aux concours nationaux, soulignant qu’une baisse des taux de réussite avait également été enregistrée dans des pays voisins, et estimant que les résultats obtenus cette année reflètent les niveaux réels des candidats. Les deux concours avaient débuté le mois dernier, avec la participation de plus de 119 000 élèves au concours d’entrée en première année de collège « concours » et d’environ 75 000 candidats au concours de fin d’études de collège «brevet ».

items.title: Éducation : L’école primaire publique de Loyargo, dans l’Oubritenga, fait un 100 % au CEP
items.description: Éducation : L'école primaire publique de Loyargo, dans l'Oubritenga, fait un 100 % au CEP Ourgou-Manega, 14 juillet 2026 (AIB) – L'École primaire publique de Loyargo, dans la commune de Ourgou-Manéga, province du Bassitenga, a clôturé, ce lundi 13 juillet 2026, ses activités pédagogiques de l'année scolaire 2025-2026 au cours d'une cérémonie présidée par le Chef de la Circonscription d'éducation de base (CCEB), en présence d'une communauté éducative fortement mobilisée. La cérémonie a également été marquée par une initiative de l'Amicale des anciens élèves de l'École primaire publique de Loyargo, qui a procédé à une plantation d'arbres dans l'enceinte de l'établissement pour contribuer à la protection de l'environnement et à l'amélioration du cadre de vie scolaire. En reconnaissance des efforts consentis tout au long de l'année, des attestations de félicitations ont été remises aux enseignants. Les meilleurs élèves ont, quant à eux, reçu des récompenses pour saluer leurs efforts. « Nous remercions tous les acteurs qui ont travaillé jour et nuit pour ces résultats. Nous sommes très fiers et nous serons toujours disponibles pour accompagner les activités de l'école », a déclaré Adama Ouédraogo, le président de l'Amicale. À cette occasion, le directeur de l'école, le camarade Olivier Doulkom, a présenté le bilan de l'année scolaire, marqué par un taux de réussite de 100 % au Certificat d'études primaires (CEP). Les 35 candidats présentés à l'examen ont tous été admis, un résultat identique à celui enregistré l'année précédente. Le CCEB de Ourgou-Manéga, le camarade Jean-Baptiste Ouédraogo, a salué cette performance, qu'il a attribuée à l'engagement du personnel enseignant, à la détermination des élèves ainsi qu'à l'accompagnement des parents d'élèves et des partenaires de l'école. Il a invité les différents acteurs à maintenir cette dynamique afin de consolider les acquis. Pour rappel, l'école primaire publique de Loyargo a ouvert ses portes en 2004. Agence d'Information du Burkina S.O/LC
